origin.name: Burundi
origin.key_document: Acte de deces (death certificate in French, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Burundi travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Bujumbura guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is not widely available in Burundi. Full body repatriation is the standard approach.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Acte de deces (certified English translation required)
special_rules: All French-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Burundian authorities is required for all repatriations.
The FCDO advises checking current travel advice before arranging logistics.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Bujumbura can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Burundi to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Burundi. Repatriation to the UK takes 3-5 weeks. Civil registry, French translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
